mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, depressing equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in residential housing starts indicates supply chain bottlenecks colliding with shifting demand composition, creating earnings revision risk that suppresses equity prices over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 1.0% month-over-month while HOUST rises > 5.0% month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in at least 50% of historical instances where both conditions align, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid rate hikes, rising real estate activity reflects resilient credit demand that offsets tightening headwinds, supporting positive equity returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns turn negative when housing starts rise during rate hike periods, or the forward return distribution shows no mean difference between rising and falling housing periods in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly tracks corporate profit expansion, providing a continuous positive signal for equity valuations as earnings revisions move in tandem with macroeconomic output.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between quarterly GDP growth and 60-day forward SPX returns drops below 0.10 for three consecutive years, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during GDP contraction quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for three consecutive months, it confirms robust labor demand that fuels wage growth and consumption, driving positive equity returns over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 200,000 for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: The signal generates negative or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 40% of qualifying episodes, or the predictive rank correlation falls below 0.05 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances combined with stable or falling inflation indicate healthy real purchasing power expansion, which lifts equity valuations over a two-week horizon as earnings expectations upgrade.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month change > 2.0% while CPIAUCSL month-over-month change < 0.3%
failure_condition: Equity returns decline when credit growth accelerates alongside low inflation, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing where credit expansion fails to translate into earnings beats.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle environment where industrial output peaks and unemployment begins ticking up, supply-side constraints finally meet weakening demand, triggering a sharp equity drawdown over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO peaks (3-month moving average declines) while UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
failure_condition: The S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in the majority of instances where industrial production peaks and unemployment rises, or the forward return median exceeds zero during these specific macro conditions.
